title: Labguru API Schemas
description: JSON Schema definitions for the Labguru REST API

account_id.description: (optional) The account id to generate the token for

item[attachment].description: The file that will be uploaded.

item[title].description: The title of the attachment. This should match the file name exactly, including the file extension (.xlsx, .csv, etc.).

item[attach_to_uuid].description:
The UUID of the object to which the attachment will be linked.
If this parameter is not provided, the uploaded file will be stored but not associated with any specific entity (unattached file).

item[description].description: A brief description of the attachment content.

element_id.description: The ID of the attachment element to which the attachment will be linked

alternative_name.description: An alternative name for the worm

gene_id.description: An ID reference to a gene from your labguru genes collection

phenotype.description: Observable physical or biochemical characteristics of the worm

mutagen.description: The agent used to induce mutations in the worm

growth_conditions.description: Specific conditions under which the worm is maintained

outcrossed.description: Number of times the worm strain has been outcrossed with another

dauer_defective.description:
Indicates whether the worm is defective in entering or exiting the dauer stage,
                    with '1' for yes and '0' for no.

owner_id.description: The ID of the owner - by default it's your member id

alternative_name.description: An additional name for the antibody

clone_field.description: The clone from which the antibody was derived.

isotype.description: The class or type of the antibody (e.g., IgG, IgM).

preparation_date.description: The date on which the antibody was prepared, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.

source.description: The origin or source from which the antibody was obtained.

immune.description: Indicates the antibody's clonality: "Monoclonal" (immune = 1), "Polyclonal" (immune = 0), or "None" (Empty value).

organism_id.description: The ID for the organism in which the antibody was raised. This ID corresponds to specific organisms (e.g., 5 for Mouse, 6 for Chicken, etc.).

reacts_with.description: Specifies the organism or group the antibody is reactive with, chosen from a predefined list of options such as "Mouse", "Human", "Chicken", and "Zebrafish", etc.

description.description: A detailed description of the antibody

milestone_id.description: You can also provide a milestone_name(string) instead of milestone_id(integer)

protocol_id.description: Start experiment from protocol by providing the protocol id

alternative_name.description: Any alternative name by which the plasmid may be known

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id

length.description: The total number of base pairs (bp) in the plasmid’s DNA sequence.

usage.description: Intended or common uses of the plasmid (cloning, gene expression, etc.)

host.description: Typical host organism for the plasmid

resistance.description: Antibiotic resistance markers present in the plasmid

clone_number.description: Unique identifier or clone number associated with the plasmid

source.description: Origin or provider of the plasmid

sequence.description: The nucleotide sequence of the plasmid

description.description: A detailed description of the plasmid

comment.description: The text content of the comment

commentable_id.description: The id of the item to which the comment is being added

commentable_type.description: Specifies the class name of the item to which the comment is attached, such as Biocollections::Plasmid

source_id.description: The ID of the source item

source_type.description: Specifies the class name of the source item, such as Biocollections::Plasmid

target_id.description: The ID of the target item

target_type.description: Specifies the class name of the target item, such as Biocollections::Antibody

source_uuid.description: The UUID of the source item

target_uuid.description: The UUID of the target item

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id

name.description: The name of the Rodent cage

parent_id.description: The ID of the storage location

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id
id of the owner - by default it's your member id

source.description: The origin or source from which the gene was obtained.

gene_insert.description: Specific gene or genetic insert present in the virus

plasmid.description: The plasmid that is used in the creation or manipulation of the virus

serotype_strain.description: serotype or strain of the virus

mutations_deletions.description: genetic mutations or deletions within the virus details

species.description: The species from which the virus was isolated or generated

storage_conditions.description: Conditions under which the virus should be stored

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id
ID of the owner (default: your member id)

alternative_name.description: Any alternative name by which the lipid may be known

molecular_formula.description: The molecular formula of the lipid

conditions_for_use.description: Recommended conditions under which the lipid should be used

conditions_for_storage.description: Guidelines for proper storage of the lipid

safety_information.description: Safety guidelines and hazard information related to the lipid

impurities.description: Details of known impurities found in the lipid sample

reproduction.description: Details of the mode of reproduction for the yeast

owner_id.description: id of the owner - if omitted, will default to your member id
ID of the owner (default: your member id)

sensitivity.description: Details the bacterium’s sensitivity to antibiotics or other agents.

strain.description: The specific strain of the bacterium

source.description: The origin or isolation source of the bacterium (e.g., environmental, clinical).

owner_id.description: The ID of the owner - by default it's your member id

description.description: A detailed description of the bacterium

alternative_name.description: Any alternative name by which the primer may be known

owner_id.description: id of the owner - if omitted, will default to your member id

sequence.description: The nucleotide sequence of the primer

tag.description: A unique label or tag associated with the primer

gene_id.description: A reference ID linking the primer to a specific gene in your lab

tm.description: The melting temperature (Tm) of the primer

orientation.description: Indicates the orientation of the primer. Set "1" for forward and "0" for reverse. This field is optional and can be left null if orientation is not applicable

target_position.description: The specific binding position of the primer on the target DNA

fragment_size.description: The size of the DNA fragment that the primer is designed to amplify

organism.description: The specific organism for which the primer is designed, such as "Mouse"

used_for.description: The specific applications for which the primer is used (pcr / RT / SDM / etc.)

restriction_site.description: Information about any restriction enzyme recognition sites incorporated into the primer design

source.description: The origin or source from which the primer was obtained

description.description: A detailed description of the primer

owner_id.description: id of the owner - if omitted, will default to your member id

phenotype.description: Observable physical or biochemical characteristics of the seed

male_parent_id.description: The ID of the male parent plant from your Labguru plants collection, if applicable.

female_parent_id.description: The ID of the female parent plant from your Labguru plants collection, if applicable.

name.description: The name of the cell line

owner_id.description: The ID of the owner - by default it's your member id

organism.description: The organism from which the cells were originated

tissue.description: The tissue from which the cells were extracted

medium_and_serum.description: A supplement used for cultivating cells

source.description: The location or environment from which the cells were originated

description.description: A detailed description of the cell line

owner_id.description: ID of the owner - if omitted, will default to your id

alternative_name.description: An additional name for the protein

gene.description: The gene from which the protein is derived

species.description: The species from which the protein originates

lg_mutations.description: List of mutations in the protein structure

chemical_modifications.description: Details any chemical modifications the protein has undergone

tag.description: A label or tag attached to the protein

purification_method.description: The method used to purify the protein from other biological components

mw.description: The molecular weight of the protein

storage_buffer.description: The buffer solution in which the protein is stored

storage_temperature.description: The temperature in which the protein is stored

sequence.description: The amino acid sequence of the protein

description.description: A detailed description of the protein

name.description: The botanical name of the plant.

owner_id.description: ID of the owner - if omitted, will default to your id

phenotype.description: Observable physical or biochemical characteristics of the plant

genotype.description: The genetic makeup of the plant

harvest_date.description: The date when the plant was harvested, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id
id of the owner - by default it's your member id

seq.description: The actual sequence data in characters

kind.description: sequence type: DNA = 1, CDNA = 2, RNA = 3, PROBE = 4, PROTEIN = 5

accsesion.description: The unique accession number assigned to the sequence

organism.description: The organism from which the sequence is derived

name.description: The name of the rodent specimen

status.description: the status of the rodent: alive, dead, missing, sacrificed

alternative_name.description: additional name for the rodent specimen

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id

sex.description: sex of the rodent: male, female, unknown

name.description: The name of the maintenance type
The new name of the maintenance type

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id

source.description: The origin or source from which the fungus was obtained.

alternative_name.description: An alternative name for the tissue

species.description: The species from which the tissue is derived

animal_details.description: Detailed information about the animal from which the tissue was harvested

harvest_date.description: Harvest date - in the following format (yyyy-mm-dd)

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id
id of the owner - by default it's your member id

maintenance_type_id.description: the id of the maintenance type

frequency_frame.description: one of the following time frames - hour/day/week/month/year
one of the following time frames - hour/day/week/month/year

section_id.description: the experiment procedure id to copy

data.description: cover page data in HTML format

project_id.description: the project id to add note to

web.description: link to the company web page

data.description: templates fields in the follwing json format
templates fields in the follwing json format

container_id.description: the id of the experiment/protocol/report the section belongs to

container_type.description: the type of the container - Projects::Experiment | Knowledgebase::Protocol | Knowledgebase::AbstractDocument

container_id.description: the id of the experiment/protocol/report the section belongs to

container_type.description: The container type of the section

list.description:
An array of section IDs.
The order of IDs in this list defines the new arrangement of sections on the page

storage_type_id.description:
The ID of the storage type.
Examples include: 1 (Room), 21 (Shelf), 61 (Closet), 81 (Drawer), 111 (Cage), 120 (Refrigerator), 121 (Freezer), 201 (Cryo container), 251 (Slide Rack), 322 (Rack), 321 (Other).

item_id.description: the id of the item to to tag

class_name.description: the item type to remove the tag from

item_id.description: the id of the item to to remove the tag from

notify_members.description: who should be notified regarding the event: notify only me =[1], notify all participants = [-1], none =[0]

notify_at.description: when to notify about the event

repeat_frame.description: the time frame which the event will be repeated at - Never, days, weeks, months

eventable_id.description: the equipment id which the event is related to

item_type.description: the item type to attach the dataset to

vector_data.description: fields must be same as column headers in the dataset {"data_header": "item_name"}
fields must be same as column headers in the dataset - {"data_header": "item_name"}

description.description: Text description for this generic item

collection_type.description: The type of collection, specified in singular form, such as - Biocollections::Antibody

item_id.description: The id of the item to be added to the shopping list

quantity.description: The number of units of the item to be added to the shopping list.

remarks.description: Additional comments or special instructions related to the item.

collection_type.description: The name of the custom collection to which the item belongs.

collection_id.description: The id of the custom collection within the system.

item_id.description: The id of the item to be added to the shopping list

quantity.description: The number of units of the item to be added to the shopping list.

remarks.description: Additional comments or special instructions related to the item.

produce_by.description: [required] - in case material is non commercial

storage_id.description: type of storages: 1 - Room, 21 - Shelf, 61 - Closet, 81 - Drawer, 111 - Cage, 120 - Refrigerator ,121 - Freezer, 181 - Rack Cell , 201 - Cryo container, 251 - Slide Rack, 261 - Rack Cell, 322 - Rack, 323 - Rack Cell, 321 - Other

storage_type.description: storage class name - [Box - System::Storage::Box] [all other storages type - System::Storage::Storage]

stockable_type.description: class name of the stockable item type, such as: Biocollections::Antibody

location_in_box.description: if storage is grid box, indicates numerical location in box

owner_id.description: id of the owner - by default it's your member id

request_template_id.description: The ID of the request template to start the request from

priority.description: Specifies the priority level of the request. By default - Low

status.description: The request status, indicating the current state of the request.

priority.description: Specifies the priority level of the request. By default - Low
Specifies the priority level of the request. By default - Low
Specifies the priority level of the request. By default - Low

performed_by.description: The ID of the member which will perform the request
The ID of the member which will perform the request

name.description: The name of the request template
The name of the request template

value.description: Filter value - Date in the following format 'YYYY-MM-DD'

id.description: User id in the following format "[1]"

dataset_id.description: The ID of the dataset to which the visualization will be linked to

attachment_id.description: The ID of the attachment (image) that will appear under the 'Visualization' tab of the dataset

item.description:
Valid element types are:
• samples
• equipment
• code
• compound
• reaction
• text
• steps
• excel
• plate

name.description: Specifies the name of the element. If not provided, the element will be assigned a default name

container_id.description: The ID of the section to which the element will be added

container_type.description: The type of container in which the element will be placed

element_type.description: The type of element to be added

form_json.description:
A JSON string containing key-value pairs that specify updates to the form fields.
                  Each key corresponds to a form field name and the value specifies the new content for that field.

item.description:
Valid plate dimensions are:
• 3x2 (6 wells)
• 4x3 (12 wells)
• 6x4 (24 wells)
• 8x6 (48 wells)
• 12x6 (72 wells)
• 12x8 (96 wells)
• 24x16 (384 wells)

name.description: Specifies the name of the element. If not provided, the element will be assigned a default name

container_id.description: The ID of the section to which the element will be added

container_type.description: The type of container in which the plate element will be placed

element_type.description: The type of element to be added

cols.description: the number of columns in the plate layout

rows.description: The number of rows in the plate layout

createFixedSamplePoolingEvent.title: Create fixed sample pooling event
createFixedSamplePoolingEvent.description: draw a fixed amount from all stocks.

unit.description:
Valid units of measurement are:

- For 'volume': 'nL', 'µL', 'mL', 'L'
- For 'weight': 'ng', 'µg', 'mg', 'g', 'Kg'

fixed_amount.description: Specifies the exact amount to draw

stocks_list.description: List of stock IDs from which to draw

pooled_stock_info.description:
Contains all the necessary stock fields for creating a stock during the pooling event.
                    This object may include all the relevant stock properties.

stockable_type.description:
Identifies the class name of the stock item, such as 'Biocollections::Bacterium'.
                      This indicates the category of the entity and must match valid system-defined class names.

storage_type.description:
Specifies the storage classification, such as 'System::Storage::Storage',
                        indicating the type of storage facility used for the item.

createCustomSamplePoolingEvent.title: Create custom sample pooling event
createCustomSamplePoolingEvent.description: draw a custom amount from each stock.

unit.description:
Valid units of measurement are:

- For 'volume': 'nL', 'µL', 'mL', 'L'
- For 'weight': 'ng', 'µg', 'mg', 'g', 'Kg'

stocks_usage.description: Hash of stock IDs and the desired amount to pool.

additionalProperties.description: Amount to be pooled for the given stock ID.

pooled_stock_info.description:
Contains all the necessary stock fields for creating a stock during the pooling event.
                    This object may include all the relevant stock properties.

stockable_type.description:
Identifies the class name of the stock item, such as 'Biocollections::Bacterium'.
                        This indicates the category of the entity and must match valid system-defined class names.

storage_type.description:
Specifies the storage classification, such as 'System::Storage::Storage',
                        indicating the type of storage facility used for the item.

createConsumeSamplePoolingEvent.title: Create consume sample pooling event
createConsumeSamplePoolingEvent.description: draw the entire amount and consume.

unit.description:
Valid units of measurement are:

- For 'volume': 'nL', 'µL', 'mL', 'L'
- For 'weight': 'ng', 'µg', 'mg', 'g', 'Kg'

stocks_list.description: List of stock IDs from which to draw

pooled_stock_info.description:
Contains all the necessary stock fields for creating a stock during the pooling event.
                    This object may include all the relevant stock properties.

stockable_type.description:
Identifies the class name of the stock item, such as 'Biocollections::Bacterium'.
                      This indicates the category of the entity and must match valid system-defined class names.

storage_type.description: Specifies the storage classification, such as 'System::Storage::Storage', indicating the type of storage facility used for the item.

title.description: Specifies the name of the flag

message.example: You are not authorized to perform this action
